claim: AP2 (Agent Payments Protocol) is a Google-backed extension of A2A and MCP for agents to safely make payments on user behalf
actionable: Monitor AP2 adoption for building agents that can autonomously purchase items; requires cryptographic intent verification

claim: AP2 defines 'human present' (agent finds options, human approves) vs 'human not present' (agent autonomously buys with cryptographically signed intent mandate) modes
actionable: Start with human-present mode for safer deployments; cryptographically signed intent is required for autonomous purchases

claim: AP2 implements 'verifiable intent not inferred action' principle: transactions must be anchored in deterministic, cryptographically-provable user intent, not ambiguous LLM outputs
actionable: For financial agent workflows, design intent capture mechanisms that generate cryptographic proofs of user authorization before agent acts

claim: AP2 prioritizes user privacy by design and defines clear liability boundaries between user, agent, merchant, and payment processor
actionable: When implementing AP2, understand liability boundaries; document who is responsible if transactions fail or go wrong

claim: Future agentic commerce will enable agent app stores where specialized agents negotiate prices and execute tasks for other agents, settling in micropayments
actionable: Anticipate agent marketplaces; design agents with API-first architecture to support machine-to-machine negotiation and payment flows
